news.title: STAT+: Eli Lilly dives into hair loss treatments with investment in AI startup Absci
news.summary: Eli Lilly is investing in a small startup developing a medication to spur hair growth, and potentially also treat endometriosis.

news.title: STAT+: U.S. health spending rose sharply in 2025, thanks to GLP-1 use and more care
news.summary: People are getting more care and using lots of GLP-1 drugs, fueling a sharp increase in health care spending.

news.title: Judge temporarily blocks subpoenas in criminal probe of transgender care at New York hospitals
news.summary: A judge temporarily blocked federal prosecutors in Texas from getting access to the medical records of transgender patients treated at New York hospitals on Wednesday.

news.title: Clinical trial set to test two drugs for fast-growing Ebola outbreak
news.summary: A clinical trial testing two drugs against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus, which is driving a fast-moving outbreak in Central Africa, is set to begin next week, WHO officials said.

news.title: STAT+: A dispatch on AI from BIOtech’s big summer bash
news.summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: Brittany Trang brings the latest from BIO on how biotech companies are approaching artificial intelligence.

news.title: STAT+: AI wades into a vexing medical mystery: What causes sudden cardiac death?
news.summary: A new study published in Nature uses artificial intelligence to identify people at high risk for sudden cardiac death, and pinpoints a possible reason.

news.title: STAT+: At BIO, a glimpse behind Lilly and Chai’s partnership
news.summary: RIP Sangamo, onshoring with drug manufacturing, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about an FDA commissioner candidate, Germany’s plans for drug prices, and more
news.summary: Regenxbio plans to apply for accelerated approval for its gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

news.title: STAT+: Wagering on FDA changes, Regenxbio will submit Duchenne gene therapy for approval
news.summary: Regenxbio plans to submit its Duchenne gene therapy for FDA approval, just a month after it said that regulators wanted to see another trial.

news.title: FDA finally analyzed its own youth tobacco data
news.summary: Government can't restrict SNAP dollars on candy, diminishing returns for IVF add-ons, and other health news

news.title: STAT+: Ophthalmology venture grabs investors’ attention, raises $330 million
news.summary: A startup developing treatments for eye diseases has raised $330 million from investors as it readies for late-stage testing.

news.title: Opinion: How I used public radio to recruit 20,000 participants for a peer-reviewed study on walking breaks
news.summary: “With the right structure, Americans will roll up their sleeves, do the science, and trust the result,” Manoush Zomorodi writes.

news.title: Opinion: New human embryo editing advances require tough conversations on ethical boundaries
news.summary: Scientists can’t ignore the ethical complexities of new embryo editing advances.

news.title: STAT+: What an abandoned heart failure trial says about ‘breakthrough’ devices that don’t reach patients
news.summary: An abandoned heart-failure trial offers a rare window into devices that the FDA deems "breakthrough" but never reach patients.

news.title: STAT+: FDA drops enforcement against Whoop after it tweaks blood pressure feature
news.summary: The FDA told wearable maker Whoop last week that it would not take further enforcement action over the company's blood pressure feature.

news.title: Assort Health scores $120M series C to scale voice AI agent platform for healthcare
news.summary: Assort Health has a large proprietary specialty dataset built on 190 million patient interactions, 62,000 care protocols and 1.6 million decision pathways.

news.title: Kaiser Health Plan ordered to pay hospital $82M over insufficient reimbursement
news.summary: A drawn-out legal back-and-forth between Kaiser Health Plan and Pomona Valley Hospital over out-of-network emergency care reimbursement hinged on the terms of a previously terminated contract, making the case "a useful reminder that a terminated contract is not always a dead document.”

news.title: Florida AG to probe CVS for anticompetitive pharmacy practices
news.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has issued a subpoena to CVS Health, citing concern about potential anticompetitive practices from the vertically integrated healthcare giant.

news.title: US health spending set to reach $9T by 2034: CMS report
news.summary: U.S. healthcare spending is set to climb to $9 trillion by 2034, representing 20.6% of the economy, according to new federal data. Actuaries cited prescription drugs as a near-term driver of utilization and cost.

news.title: Locum tenens coverage becoming ‘more purposeful’ staffing strategy: Definitive Healthcare
news.summary: Definitive Healthcare identified four key workforce trends around contingent staffing, advanced practice providers, new behavioral health delivery models and AI tech as a workforce multiplier.

news.title: Judge rebuffs DOJ's grand jury subpoena for info on NYU Langone's trans patients
news.summary: NYU Langone Health had disclosed in May that it was "one of several institutions" facing a criminal investigation over its delivery of the services to minors, an escalation in the Trump administration's pressure campaign.

news.title: Senators call for $50B rural health fund to better target small providers, relax spending restrictions
news.summary: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services has substantial leeway on how funds from the Rural Health Transformation Program are doled out. Lawmakers warn the current approach "may unintentionally disadvantage many of the rural hospitals and clinics the program was intended to support.”

news.title: Some life insurers compete with health insurers on benefits. Here's why
news.summary: Life insurers are intimately familiar with death and its causes. Some have decided to help customers be proactive about their health.

news.title: Patients prefer healthcare providers' AI agents to public chatbots, with human oversight non‑negotiable, survey finds
news.summary: Patients are open to using AI agents to help with tasks like scheduling and billing, but they overwhelmingly prefer AI tools that live inside their doctor’s secure portal, a new Salesforce survey found.

news.title: Coval raises $28M series A to address AI voice agent reliability, compliance
news.summary: The company plans to use the funds to address deployment reliability and compliance issues, expand its sales and solutions engineering teams and advance product capabilities.

news.title: Badges, bots and blind spots
news.summary: Hospitals are under pressure to secure increasingly digital care environments without slowing clinicians down. This week on "Podnosis," Senior Writer Anastassia Gliadkovskaya talks with Imprivata Chief Medical Officer Sean Kelly, M.D., about access management, AI risk and the blind spots hospitals need to watch as new tools enter their systems.

news.title: DOJ announces $6.5B healthcare fraud takedown with record Medicaid enforcement
news.summary: Law enforcement unveiled charges against 455 defendants for their alleged participation in healthcare schemes, among which fraudulent amniotic wound allografts and undelivered Medicaid services were spotlighted.

news.title: Spurned by FDA, Passage Bio finds exit through merger with Remix
news.summary: Passage Bio, the Philadelphia-based gene therapy outfit that launched a strategic review earlier this year after tough feedback from the FDA, has found an exit in an all-stock reverse merger with RNA specialist Remix Therapeutics.

news.title: Icon names Microsoft tech partner as Q1 results grow analyst confidence
news.summary: Irish CRO giant Icon this week made Microsoft its technology partner as the organization advances its plan to increase investment in AI over the next three years. Microsoft’s Copilot and data infrastructure will be implemented enterprise-wide to further scale Icon’s agentic AI platform, Orbis.

news.title: Mapp, Gilead antivirals to be deployed in DRC Ebola trial: WHO chief
news.summary: Officials are planning to study Mapp Biopharmaceutical’s MBP134 and Gilead Sciences’ remdesivir, which gained prominence during the COVID-19 pandemic, against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus.

news.title: Nature retracts provocative PD-1 study that tied lung cancer survival to treatment timing
news.summary: After a four-month investigation, Nature Medicine has decided to pull a viral study that linked early-day PD-1/chemo treatment to a massive survival benefit versus late-day infusion in non-small cell lung cancer.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: ADC Tx slims workforce by 17%; Sangamo sheds staff
news.summary: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: RQ Bio reels in $115M to take long-acting flu prevention antiviral into clinic
news.summary: Amid a bustling fundraising week for biotechs, the U.K.’s RQ Bio is adding $115 million to its coffers to advance a long-acting antibody for flu prevention.

news.title: Novartis pays Antares $105M to illuminate undruggable cancer targets
news.summary: Novartis is paying Antares Therapeutics $105 million upfront in a deal worth up to $1.9 billion to generate small molecules against historically undruggable oncology targets.

news.title: Abbott pens blood-based Alzheimer's testing pact with ALZpath
news.summary: Abbott has become the latest company to team up with Alzheimer’s disease blood test developer ALZpath.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker '26: Ollin hauls in $330M; RQ Bio raises $115M
news.summary: The Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker records venture capital rounds of $50 million or more secured by biotechs.

news.title: BIO 2026: Flagship inks research collaboration with Saudi sovereign wealth fund company
news.summary: On Tuesday afternoon at the 2026 Biotechnology Innovation Organization conference in San Diego, Flagship Pioneering announced a research collaboration with Lean Business Services within the well-appointed Saudi Arabia pavilion in the heart of the conference’s exhibition floor.

news.title: Insulet hires Convatec, Eli Lilly alum as new R&D chief
news.summary: Omnipod maker Insulet has hired Divakar Ramakrishnan, Ph.D., as its new Chief Research and Development Officer.

news.title: Eli Lilly reunites with Abbisko in $1.9B, multiple target R&D pact
news.summary: Eli Lilly has expanded its relationship with Abbisko Therapeutics, committing up to $1.9 billion to get the Chinese biotech to apply its drug discovery and early development capabilities to multiple targets.

news.title: 主题圆桌：生命的新算法，AI重构医疗产业全链路｜36氪WAVES2026新浪潮
news.summary: WAVES2026圆桌：AI+医疗，探讨产业落地下一步

papers.title: EBench: Elemental Diagnosis of Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies
papers.summary: We present EBench, a simulation benchmark that diagnoses generalist mobile manipulation policies beyond a single success-rate scalar. EBench comprises 26 diverse and challenging manipulation tasks annotated along 5 capability dimensions and 4 generalization dimensions. We evaluate state-of-the-art generalist manipulation models including π_0, π_{0.5}, XVLA, and InternVLA-A1, and reveal that models with near success rates exhibit strikingly different capability profiles: π_{0.5} achieves the highest test success rate and the best train--test retention, whereas InternVLA-A1 dominates mobile mani

papers.title: QG-MIL: A Gated Transformer Aggregator for Domain-Agnostic Multiple Instance Learning in Medical Imaging
papers.summary: Attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregators in medical imaging are prone to attention concentration, producing overconfident and unstable predictions. We introduce QG-MIL, a gated transformer aggregator that addresses this through four synergistic architectural components: RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward modules. Together, these design choices stabilize training and distribute attention more uniformly across instances without auxiliary losses, masking, or multi-stage regularization. We ev

papers.title: LingxiDiagBench: A Multi-Agent Framework for Benchmarking LLMs in Chinese Psychiatric Consultation and Diagnosis
papers.summary: Mental disorders are highly prevalent worldwide, but the shortage of psychiatrists and the inherent subjectivity of interview-based diagnosis create substantial barriers to timely and consistent mental-health assessment. Progress in AI-assisted psychiatric diagnosis is constrained by the absence of benchmarks that simultaneously provide realistic patient simulation, clinician-verified diagnostic labels, and support for dynamic multi-turn consultation. We present LingxiDiagBench, a large-scale multi-agent benchmark that evaluates LLMs on both static diagnostic inference and dynamic multi-turn p

papers.title: When Agents Commit Too Soon: Diagnosing Premature Commitment in LLM Agents
papers.summary: Long-horizon LLM agents can fail quietly: they settle on one reading of the evidence early, then spend the rest of the run defending it. We call this premature commitment. Final-answer scoring misses the failure mode because it sees only the answer, not whether the process has already collapsed to a stable path. We define representational commitment as cross-run hidden-state convergence at a fixed reasoning step, and use it as an early diagnostic of trajectory consistency. On Llama-3.1-70B running ReAct on HotpotQA, step-4 hidden-state similarity predicts downstream behavioral consistency (r =
